# waveform-playlist

> Multitrack Web Audio editor and player with canvas waveform visualization, built with React and Tone.js.

## What It Does

waveform-playlist is a React component library for building browser-based audio editing applications. It provides:

- Multitrack audio playback, mixing, and editing
- Canvas-based waveform and spectrogram rendering with zoom
- Click-to-seek, drag-to-select, clip drag/trim/split
- 20 real-time audio effects (reverb, delay, EQ, compression, etc.) via Tone.js
- Time-synchronized annotations with drag editing
- Recording with live waveform preview and VU level metering (input and output)
- WAV export with offline rendering
- BBC audiowaveform support for instant rendering of large files
- Sample rate hint for pre-computed peaks matching (`sampleRate` prop on provider)
- Theming via styled-components

## Packages

- `@waveform-playlist/browser` â€” Main package: React components, hooks, effects
- `@waveform-playlist/core` â€” Data model: ClipTrack, AudioClip, sample-based math
- `@waveform-playlist/engine` â€” Framework-agnostic timeline engine: PlaylistEngine class, pure clip/timeline operations, event emitter. Owns selection, loop, zoom, volume, selectedTrackId, and clip mutation state. Seek/play beyond audio duration is supported (silence where there's no audio).
- `@waveform-playlist/playout` â€” Audio playback: Tone.js adapter (implements PlayoutAdapter from engine), global AudioContext. Incremental track updates via updateTrack/replaceClips â€” no full playout rebuild on clip operations. Optional adapter methods: addTrack, removeTrack, updateTrack.
- `@waveform-playlist/ui-components` â€” Styled primitives, theming, virtual scrolling contexts, playhead components, PlaylistErrorBoundary, BeatsAndBarsProvider
- `@waveform-playlist/annotations` â€” Annotation parsing, rendering, keyboard controls
- `@waveform-playlist/recording` â€” Microphone access, AudioWorklet recording, VU meter, integrated recording hook, mic-unplug detection
- `@waveform-playlist/worklets` â€” Shared AudioWorklet processors: `meter-processor` (sample-accurate peak/RMS metering) and `recording-processor` (multi-channel audio capture). Auto-installed with recording package.
- `@waveform-playlist/webaudio-peaks` â€” Peak extraction from AudioBuffer
- `@waveform-playlist/loaders` â€” Lower-level audio loaders (BlobLoader, XHRLoader, LoaderFactory) for custom fetch/decode pipelines outside the React provider.
- `@waveform-playlist/media-element-playout` â€” HTMLAudioElement playback with pitch-preserving speed control, optional Web Audio routing for fades and Tone.js effect bridging
- `@waveform-playlist/midi` â€” React layer for MIDI: `useMidiTracks` hook converts .mid files to ClipTrack[] with midiNotes for piano roll visualization and SoundFont/PolySynth playback. As of v13+ the parser is re-exported from `@dawcore/midi`; React API surface is unchanged. The SoundFont can be provided or swapped after setup via `adapter.setSoundFontCache()` on the adapter returned by `createToneAdapter()` (the React provider forwards late `soundFontCache` prop changes automatically; construct caches with `SoundFontCache.fromUrl(url)`).
- `@dawcore/components` â€” Web Components for multi-track audio editing (Lit-based): `<daw-editor>`, `<daw-track>`, `<daw-clip>`, `<daw-waveform>`, `<daw-playhead>`, `<daw-ruler>`, transport buttons. Framework-agnostic â€” works in any framework or vanilla HTML. No Tone.js dependency; uses native Web Audio via `@dawcore/transport`. Consumer supplies a `PlayoutAdapter` (no default). Unified effects chains: `addEffect`/`removeEffect`/`setEffectParams`/`setEffectBypassed`/`moveEffect`/`effects` on `<daw-track>` (per-track chain) and `<daw-editor>` (master chain), with built-in `native-*` effects (gain, lowpass filter, compressor, stereo panner, delay), custom registration via `registerEffect`, and `daw-effect-add/remove/change/bypass/reorder` events. WAM 2.0 plugins join the same chains via `addWamPlugin(url)` (optional `@dawcore/wam` peer).
- `@dawcore/midi` â€” Framework-agnostic MIDI parser (`parseMidiFile`, `parseMidiUrl` â€” pure functions, no React). Used by `@dawcore/components` via dynamic import inside `editor.loadMidi()`, and re-exported by `@waveform-playlist/midi`.
- `@waveform-playlist/spectrogram` â€” Optional spectrogram package (v13+): React `SpectrogramProvider` + UI (menu items, settings modal) integrating via `SpectrogramIntegrationContext`. Computation/worker/orchestrator are imported from `@dawcore/spectrogram`.
- `@dawcore/spectrogram` â€” Framework-agnostic FFT computation, Web Worker, and `SpectrogramOrchestrator` class (viewport classification, 3-tier render, generation-based abort, contiguous chunk grouping, color LUT cache). Shared between the React Provider and the dawcore Lit element layer.
- `@dawcore/transport` â€” Native Web Audio transport (zero dependencies): scheduler, clock, tempo/meter map, metronome, loop. Replaces Tone.js Transport for the dawcore stack. Implements the `PlayoutAdapter` interface from `@waveform-playlist/engine` via `NativePlayoutAdapter`. Exposes effects insertion hooks (`connectTrackOutput`/`connectMasterOutput`) used by the dawcore effects chains.
- `@dawcore/wam` â€” Web Audio Modules (WAM 2.0) plugin hosting: idempotent host initialization (`ensureWamHost`) and plugin loading/validation (`createWamInstance`). Optional peer of `@dawcore/components` â€” dynamic-imported by `addWamPlugin()`, so non-WAM users load zero WAM bytes.

## Quick Start

```tsx
import { WaveformPlaylistProvider, Waveform, PlayButton, PauseButton, StopButton, useAudioTracks } from '@waveform-playlist/browser';

function App() {
  const { tracks, loading } = useAudioTracks([
    { src: '/audio/track1.mp3', name: 'Track 1' },
    { src: '/audio/track2.mp3', name: 'Track 2' },
  ]);

  if (loading) return <div>Loading...</div>;

  return (
    <WaveformPlaylistProvider tracks={tracks} timescale controls={{ show: true, width: 200 }}>
      <PlayButton /> <PauseButton /> <StopButton />
      <Waveform />
    </WaveformPlaylistProvider>
  );
}
```

## Architecture

Both providers use React Context with 4 split contexts for performance.

**WaveformPlaylistProvider** (multi-track, Web Audio API):
1. **usePlaybackAnimation()** â€” High-frequency: isPlaying, currentTime, timing refs
2. **usePlaylistState()** â€” Medium-frequency: selection, loop, annotations, settings
3. **usePlaylistControls()** â€” Stable: play/pause/stop/seekTo, track controls, zoom, settings setters
4. **usePlaylistData()** â€” Stable: duration, tracks, trackStates, playoutRef (PlaylistEngine), isDraggingRef, display config, isReady

**MediaElementPlaylistProvider** (single-track, HTMLAudioElement):
1. **useMediaElementAnimation()** â€” isPlaying, currentTime
2. **useMediaElementState()** â€” continuousPlay, annotations, playbackRate
3. **useMediaElementControls()** â€” play/pause/stop/seekTo, setPlaybackRate
4. **useMediaElementData()** â€” duration, peaksDataArray, sampleRate, display config

## Key Hooks

- `useAudioTracks(configs, options?)` â€” Load audio files into ClipTrack[] for the provider (declarative, configs-driven). Options: `{ immediate: true }` renders placeholder tracks instantly while audio decodes
- `useDynamicTracks()` â€” Imperative hook for runtime track additions (drag-and-drop, file picker). Creates placeholder tracks instantly while audio decodes in parallel
- `useDynamicEffects()` â€” Master effects chain with runtime add/remove/parameter updates
- `useTrackDynamicEffects()` â€” Per-track effects management
- `useClipDragHandlers(opts)` â€” Drag-to-move and boundary trimming, delegates to engine.moveClip()/trimClip()
- `useDragSensors(opts?)` â€” Configures PointerSensor activation constraints for DragDropProvider (distance-based default, optional touch-optimized mode)
- `ClipCollisionModifier` â€” Collision detection modifier for clip moves, prevents overlapping clips
- `SnapToGridModifier` â€” Snap-to-grid modifier for clip moves. Two modes: `'beats'` (PPQN tick grid) and `'timescale'` (sample-based grid). Snaps absolute position, not delta.
- `noDropAnimationPlugins` â€” Disables Feedback plugin drop animation for clip moves
- `useClipSplitting(opts)` â€” Split clips at playhead, delegates to engine.splitClip()
- `useExportWav()` â€” Offline WAV export with effects support
- `useKeyboardShortcuts(opts)` â€” Custom keyboard shortcuts
- `usePlaybackShortcuts(opts)` â€” Default playback shortcuts (Space, Escape, 0)
- `useUndoState(opts)` â€” Undo/redo state (canUndo, canRedo, undo, redo) with engine delegation
- `KeyboardShortcuts` â€” Self-closing component for declarative keyboard shortcut setup. Props: `playback` (Space/Escape/0), `clipSplitting` ('s' key), `annotations` (arrow nav, boundary editing), `undo` (Cmd/Ctrl+Z, Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Z), `additionalShortcuts`. All booleans default to false. Reads all required data from context.

### MIDI Hooks (from `@waveform-playlist/midi`)

- `useMidiTracks(configs, options)` â€” Load .mid files into ClipTrack[] with midiNotes for piano roll rendering. `options: { sampleRate: number }`. One config can produce multiple tracks (one per MIDI channel). Supports pre-parsed notes via `midiNotes` prop.
- `parseMidiFile(data, options?)` â€” Pure function: ArrayBuffer â†’ ParsedMidi with tracks, notes, tempo, time signature. No React dependency. Re-exported from `@dawcore/midi`.
- `parseMidiUrl(url, options?, signal?)` â€” Fetch + parse convenience wrapper with AbortSignal support. Re-exported from `@dawcore/midi`.

### MIDI Loading (from `@dawcore/components`, requires optional `@dawcore/midi` peer dep)

- `editor.loadMidi(source, options?)` â€” Imperatively load a `.mid` file (URL string or File) and create N `<daw-track>` elements with `render-mode="piano-roll"` â€” one per note-bearing MIDI track. Returns `Promise<MidiLoadResult>` with `{ trackIds, bpm, timeSignature, duration, name }`. Cleanup-on-failure via `Promise.allSettled`: any per-track failure removes every track appended during the call. Dynamic-imports `@dawcore/midi` on first call; throws friendly install hint if missing.
- `MidiLoadOptions` â€” `{ startTime?: number; signal?: AbortSignal }`. `signal` is forwarded to `fetch()` only; aborting after parse is a no-op.
- `MidiLoadResult` â€” `{ readonly trackIds: readonly string[]; readonly bpm: number; readonly timeSignature: readonly [number, number]; readonly duration: number; readonly name: string }`.

### Beats & Bars (from `@waveform-playlist/ui-components`)

- `BeatsAndBarsProvider` â€” Optional context providing BPM, time signature, and snap config. Wraps playlist content to enable beats & bars timescale mode in SmartScale. Props: `bpm`, `timeSignature: [number, number]`, `snapTo: 'bar' | 'beat' | 'off'`
- `getScaleInfo(samplesPerPixel)` â€” Returns `{ marker, bigStep, smallStep }` (ms) for temporal timescale tick spacing at the given zoom level. Also useful for deriving `gridSamples` for `SnapToGridModifier` temporal mode.

### Virtual Scrolling Hooks (from `@waveform-playlist/ui-components`)

- `useScrollViewport()` â€” Current scroll position and viewport dimensions (with 1.5x overscan buffer)
- `useVisibleChunkIndices(totalWidth, chunkWidth, originX?)` â€” Memoized array of visible chunk indices for canvas virtualization
- `useClipViewportOrigin()` â€” Clip's pixel offset in the timeline (for correct chunk visibility in multi-clip tracks)

### Recording Hooks (from `@waveform-playlist/recording`)

- `useIntegratedRecording(tracks, setTracks, selectedTrackId, options)` â€” Combined recording + track management: mic access, VU meter, start/stop/pause recording, auto-adds clips to selected track
- `useRecording(stream, options)` â€” Low-level recording via AudioWorklet with live peaks
- `useMicrophoneAccess()` â€” Request mic permission, enumerate devices, get MediaStream
- `useMicrophoneLevel(stream, options?)` â€” Real-time VU meter levels from mic stream (supports multi-channel via `channelCount` option)
- `useOutputMeter(options?)` â€” Real-time master output VU meter levels (from `@waveform-playlist/browser`). Returns per-channel `levels`, `peakLevels`, and `resetPeak()`

## Key Components

- `WaveformPlaylistProvider` â€” Multi-track provider (Web Audio API / Tone.js). **Single instance per page** (shared Tone.js Transport). Props: tracks, timescale, controls, theme, effects, annotationList, onAnnotationsChange, onTracksChange, onReady, barWidth, barGap, waveHeight, samplesPerPixel, zoomLevels, mono
- `MediaElementPlaylistProvider` â€” Single-track provider (HTMLAudioElement). Multiple instances per page OK (independent audio elements). Props: track, audioContext, playbackRate, timescale, controls, theme, annotationList, onAnnotationsChange, onReady, barWidth, barGap, waveHeight, samplesPerPixel. Track config supports fadeIn/fadeOut (requires audioContext). Use for language learning, podcasts, single-track viewers, and Tone.js effect bridging.
- `Waveform` â€” Main visualization. Props: showClipHeaders, interactiveClips, renderPlayhead, renderTrackControls, showFades, touchOptimized
- `ClipInteractionProvider` â€” Declarative wrapper for clip drag/move/trim/snap/collision. Props: snap (boolean, auto-detects beats vs timescale from BeatsAndBarsProvider context), touchOptimized. Auto-enables interactiveClips on descendant Waveform. Replaces manual DragDropProvider + useClipDragHandlers + modifier setup. `useClipInteractionEnabled()` hook returns true when inside this provider.
- `KeyboardShortcuts` â€” Self-closing component for declarative keyboard shortcut setup. Props: `playback`, `clipSplitting`, `annotations`, `undo`, `additionalShortcuts`. All booleans default to false. Must be inside WaveformPlaylistProvider.
- Buttons: PlayButton, PauseButton, StopButton, RewindButton, FastForwardButton, LoopButton, SetLoopRegionButton, ClearAllButton, ZoomInButton, ZoomOutButton, ExportWavButton
- Controls: MasterVolumeControl, TimeFormatSelect, AudioPosition, SelectionTimeInputs, AutomaticScrollCheckbox, ContinuousPlayCheckbox

## Documentation

- Full docs: https://naomiaro.github.io/waveform-playlist/docs/
- LLM API reference: https://naomiaro.github.io/waveform-playlist/docs/api/llm-reference
- Engine guide (framework-agnostic): https://naomiaro.github.io/waveform-playlist/docs/guides/engine
- Examples: https://naomiaro.github.io/waveform-playlist/docs/examples
- GitHub: https://github.com/naomiaro/waveform-playlist
